name: Russian-Annexed Ukrainian Oblasts 2022 (Donetsk + Luhansk + Kherson + Zaporizhzhia)
summary: The 2022 Russian-Annexed Ukrainian Oblasts comprise four Eastern European civil-law disputed territories — Donetsk People's Republic (DPR / ДНР), Luhansk People's Republic (LPR / ЛНР), Kherson Oblast, and Zaporizhzhia Oblast — incorporated into the Russian Federation as constituent federal subjects effective 30 September 2022 by Russian Federation Constitutional Amendment, following the 23-27 September 2022 referendums (not recognised by Ukraine, the UN General Assembly, EU, US, and most other states). Structurally distinctive globally as the first simultaneous annexation of four oblasts (~108,000 km², ~6 million population per Russian claims, larger than annexed Crimea ~27,000 km² ~2.4 million) by a UN Permanent-Five Security Council member from another UN member state in modern history, and the largest single-event territorial change in post-Cold-War Europe. The annexation was preceded by Russian recognition of DPR and LPR independence on 21 February 2022 (immediately before the 24 February 2022 full-scale Russian invasion of Ukraine) — making DPR and LPR uniquely both former unrecognised de-facto states (2014-2022) and now Russian Federation constituent subjects (since 30 September 2022). The annexed oblasts are partially controlled by Russia and partially by Ukraine — Russia controls ~70-85% of DPR and LPR but only ~75% of Kherson Oblast and ~75% of Zaporizhzhia Oblast as of 2026. UNGA Resolution ES-11/4 of 12 October 2022 declared the annexation referendums invalid (143 in favour, 5 against, 35 abstentions — the strongest GA condemnation of any post-Cold-War territorial annexation). Family-law framework operates under the Russian Federation Family Code (Семейный кодекс РФ 1995) in Russian-administered areas and Ukrainian Family Code (Сімейний кодекс України 2002) in Ukrainian-controlled areas of the same oblasts — a structurally distinctive dual-family-law-framework situation. Russian-administered apex courts are the respective Republic-level Supreme Courts and Oblast Courts; Ukrainian-controlled apex courts continue under the pre-2022 Ukrainian framework. Final appellate jurisdiction in Russian-administered areas lies with the Supreme Court of the Russian Federation. The annexed oblasts are silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. Hague Convention 1980 applicability operates under contested Russian territorial extension framework (Ukraine and most states do not recognise applicability via Russian extension; Ukrainian Hague 2006 accession remains the recognised framework).

statutory_framework.citation: Russian Federation Constitutional Amendment 30 September 2022 (Article 65 incorporation)
statutory_framework.title: Russian Federation Constitutional Amendment 30 September 2022
statutory_framework.relevance: Russian Federation Constitutional Amendment incorporating Donetsk People's Republic, Luhansk People's Republic, Kherson Oblast, and Zaporizhzhia Oblast as constituent federal subjects — not recognised by Ukraine and most other states.

statutory_framework.citation: Russian Federation Family Code 1995 (applicable in annexed oblasts per Russian administration)
statutory_framework.title: Russian Federation Family Code
statutory_framework.relevance: Russian Federation Family Code chapters 11-12 governing parental rights and child custody applicable in Russian-administered annexed oblasts.

statutory_framework.citation: Ukrainian Family Code 2002 (applicable in Ukrainian-controlled areas of annexed oblasts)
statutory_framework.title: Ukrainian Family Code
statutory_framework.relevance: Ukrainian Family Code governing family-law matters in Ukrainian-controlled areas of the annexed oblasts.

statutory_framework.title: UNGA Resolution ES-11/4
statutory_framework.relevance: UN General Assembly Emergency Special Session Resolution declaring the annexation referendums invalid — 143 in favour, 5 against, 35 abstentions, strongest GA condemnation of any post-Cold-War territorial annexation.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of the Donetsk People's Republic (Russian administration)
apex_courts.role: Apex regional court for civil and criminal matters from DPR — under Russian administration, not recognised by Ukraine.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of the Luhansk People's Republic (Russian administration)
apex_courts.role: Apex regional court for civil and criminal matters from LPR — under Russian administration, not recognised by Ukraine.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of the Russian Federation
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Russian-administered annexed oblasts.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of Ukraine (Verkhovnyi Sud)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters from Ukrainian-controlled areas of the annexed oblasts.

professional_regulators.name: Russian Federation Federal Service for Surveillance in Healthcare (Roszdravnadzor) — Russian-administered areas
professional_regulators.role: Russian professional regulatory framework in Russian-administered areas.

professional_regulators.name: Ukrainian Ministry of Health — Ukrainian-controlled areas
professional_regulators.role: Ukrainian professional regulatory framework in Ukrainian-controlled areas.

anonymisation_convention: Annexed-oblast decisions are anonymised per Russian (Russian-administered) or Ukrainian (Ukrainian-controlled) court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Ukrainian independence + Soviet-collapse-+-Ukrainian-Family-Code-substantive-heritage framework
key_developments.description: Ukrainian declaration of independence 24 August 1991 + Soviet collapse — substantively distinctive Eastern European post-Soviet-Ukrainian-republic framework. Foundational substantive Ukrainian Family Code 2002 substantive heritage framework persisting through 2022 Russian annexation. Foundational pre-2014-DPR-LPR-declaration framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Crimea annexation + DPR-and-LPR-declared + Donbas-War framework
key_developments.description: Russian annexation of Crimea 18 March 2014 + Donetsk People's Republic declared 7 April 2014 + Luhansk People's Republic declared 27 April 2014 — substantively distinctive Eastern European 2014-Donbas-War-+-DPR-LPR-unrecognised-de-facto-states framework. Foundational pre-2022-full-scale-invasion framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and Minsk-Agreements-2014-2015 + 8-year-Donbas-conflict framework.

key_developments.title: Minsk II Agreements + ceasefire-+-DPR-LPR-special-status framework
key_developments.description: Minsk II Agreements 12 February 2015 — substantively distinctive Eastern European ceasefire-+-DPR-LPR-special-status-within-Ukraine framework. Substantive cross-border-jurisdiction-practice framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory through 2022 Russian withdrawal from Minsk framework + full-scale invasion.

key_developments.title: Russian-recognition-of-DPR-and-LPR-independence + full-scale-Russian-invasion framework
key_developments.description: Russian Federation recognised DPR and LPR independence 21 February 2022 — substantively distinctive globally only-modern-recognition-of-separatist-republics-immediately-prior-to-full-scale-invasion-of-parent-state framework. Russian full-scale invasion of Ukraine 24 February 2022. Foundational substantive 2022-Russia-Ukraine-War framework persisting through 21st century affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and UN Charter-violation framework.

key_developments.title: Annexation referendums + 23-27-September-2022-+-Ukrainian-non-recognition framework
key_developments.description: Annexation referendums in DPR, LPR, Kherson Oblast, and Zaporizhzhia Oblast 23-27 September 2022 — substantively distinctive globally first-simultaneous-annexation-referendums-in-four-oblasts-by-UN-Permanent-Five-Security-Council-member-from-another-UN-member-state-in-modern-history framework. Not recognised by Ukraine and most other states. Substantive constitutional-democratic-trajectory framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Russian Federation Constitutional Amendment + annexation-of-DPR-LPR-Kherson-Zaporizhzhia framework
key_developments.description: Russian Federation Constitutional Amendment 30 September 2022 incorporating DPR, LPR, Kherson Oblast, and Zaporizhzhia Oblast as constituent federal subjects — substantively distinctive globally largest-single-event-territorial-change-in-post-Cold-War-Europe framework. Substantive 108,000 km² + ~6 million population annexation framework. Foundational substantive Russian-constituent-subject framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and dual-family-law-framework.

key_developments.title: UNGA Resolution ES-11/4 + 143-in-favour-+-strongest-condemnation framework
key_developments.description: UN General Assembly Emergency Special Session Resolution ES-11/4 of 12 October 2022 declaring the annexation referendums invalid — substantively distinctive globally strongest-GA-condemnation-of-any-post-Cold-War-territorial-annexation framework (143 in favour vs 100 for Crimea Resolution 68/262, 5 against, 35 abstentions). Substantive international-law-non-recognition framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: ICC-arrest-warrant-+-Putin-Lvova-Belova-child-deportation framework
key_developments.description: International Criminal Court arrest warrant 17 March 2023 against Vladimir Putin + Maria Lvova-Belova for war crime of unlawful deportation of Ukrainian children — substantively distinctive globally only-ICC-arrest-warrant-against-UN-Permanent-Five-Security-Council-member-head-of-state framework. Substantive Hague-Convention-applicability-+-cross-border-child-protection framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Continuing-conflict + partial-Russian-administration-70-85-percent-DPR-LPR framework
key_developments.description: Continuing 2022-2024 Russia-Ukraine War with partial Russian administration of annexed oblasts (~70-85% of DPR/LPR, ~75% of Kherson/Zaporizhzhia) — substantively distinctive globally only-modern-formal-annexation-during-active-military-conflict-with-the-annexed-from-state framework. Substantive demographic and family-law-implementation disruption affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Trump-Putin-Zelenskyy-negotiations + ceasefire-discussions + continuing-partial-administration framework
key_developments.description: Trump-Putin-Zelenskyy ceasefire negotiations framework 2025 substantively significant institutional context affecting annexed oblasts framework. Continuing partial Russian administration of annexed oblasts (~75% of DPR/LPR + ~75% of Kherson/Zaporizhzhia per 2025 frontline) + Ukrainian counter-offensive framework + continuing UNGA Resolution ES-11/4 non-recognition framework substantively significant institutional context. Dual-apex-courts framework continues with Russian Federation Family Code 1995 + Ukrainian Family Code 2002 application. Substantively significant Eastern European actively-disputed-territory institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Dual-apex-courts + Supreme Court of Russia + Supreme Court of Ukraine — dual-family-law substantive register + continuing-2022-Russia-Ukraine-War + contested-Hague-applicability framework
key_developments.description: Supreme Court of the Russian Federation + DPR Supreme Court + LPR Supreme Court (Russian administration) and Supreme Court of Ukraine (Verkhovnyi Sud, Ukrainian-controlled areas) continue to apply dual-family-law framework — Russian Federation Family Code 1995 chapters 11-12 in Russian-administered areas + Ukrainian Family Code 2002 in Ukrainian-controlled areas of same oblasts. Contested Hague Convention 1980 applicability — Ukrainian 2006 accession remains internationally recognised framework. Substantive analysis.

structural_findings: Russian-Annexed Ukrainian Oblasts 2022 operate a dual-family-law-framework: Russian Federation Family Code in Russian-administered areas, Ukrainian Family Code in Ukrainian-controlled areas — places annexed oblasts in the active-conflict-disputed-territory cluster.
First simultaneous annexation of four oblasts (~108,000 km², ~6 million population) by UN Permanent-Five Security Council member is structurally distinctive globally.
Largest single-event territorial change in post-Cold-War Europe is structurally distinctive globally.

DPR and LPR uniquely both former unrecognised de-facto states (2014-2022) and now Russian Federation constituent subjects (since 2022) is structurally distinctive globally — only modern de-facto-state-to-annexed-subject transition.
Partial Russian administration (~70-85% of DPR/LPR, ~75% of Kherson/Zaporizhzhia) creates structurally distinctive partial-territorial-administration situation — unique within annexation framework.
UNGA Resolution ES-11/4 is structurally distinctive globally — strongest GA condemnation of any post-Cold-War territorial annexation (143 in favour vs 100 for Crimea Resolution 68/262).
Active-conflict status during annexation is structurally distinctive globally — only modern formal annexation during active military conflict with the annexed-from state.
Contested Hague Convention 1980 applicability under Russian extension is structurally distinctive — Ukrainian Hague 2006 accession remains the recognised framework.

sources.title: Supreme Court of the Russian Federation

editorial_notes: Russian-Annexed Ukrainian Oblasts 2022 jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 5 to 10 key_developments with full Ukrainian-independence-1991-to-continuing-2022-Russia-Ukraine-War trajectory: 1991-Ukrainian-independence + 2014-Crimea-annexation-+-DPR-LPR-declared + 2015-Minsk-II-Agreements + 2022-Russian-recognition-+-full-scale-invasion + 2022-annexation-referendums + 2022-Russian-Federation-Constitutional-Amendment + 2022-UNGA-Resolution-ES-11/4 + 2023-ICC-arrest-warrant-Putin-Lvova-Belova + 2024-continuing-conflict + 2024-dual-apex-courts-+-contested-Hague-applicability.

Civil-law Eastern European actively-disputed-territory bundle (DPR + LPR + Kherson Oblast + Zaporizhzhia Oblast) with dual-family-law-framework (Russian Federation Family Code 1995 in Russian-administered areas + Ukrainian Family Code 2002 in Ukrainian-controlled areas) + Russian Federation Constitutional Amendment 30 September 2022 + UNGA Resolution ES-11/4 invalidation + DPR/LPR de-facto-state-to-annexed-subject transition + partial-territorial-administration + active-conflict-during-annexation + contested-Hague-applicability. First simultaneous annexation of four oblasts by UN P5 member globally + largest single-event territorial change in post-Cold-War Europe + only de-facto-state-to-annexed-subject transition + only modern formal annexation during active military conflict + ICC arrest warrant against UN P5 head of state distinctive.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive dual-family-law analysis under Russian Federation Family Code 1995 chapters 11-12 (Russian-administered) + Ukrainian Family Code 2002 (Ukrainian-controlled) + contested Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Eastern-European + civil-law + actively-disputed-territory-bundle-cluster + four-oblast-simultaneous-annexation-by-UN-P5-member-globally-distinctive + largest-single-event-territorial-change-in-post-Cold-War-Europe-globally-distinctive + DPR-LPR-de-facto-state-to-annexed-subject-transition-2014-2022-globally-distinctive + partial-territorial-administration-70-85-percent + only-modern-formal-annexation-during-active-military-conflict-globally-distinctive + UNGA-Resolution-ES-11/4-143-in-favour-strongest-post-Cold-War-condemnation + ICC-arrest-warrant-Putin-Lvova-Belova-2023-child-deportation-globally-distinctive + dual-family-law-framework-Russian-+-Ukrainian + contested-Hague-applicability-Ukrainian-2006-accession-recognised + 2014-2015-Donbas-War-+-Minsk-II-Agreements + 2022-Russia-Ukraine-War clusters within the corpus.
